activities_projects.title: Rectangle Area Comparison
steps: Draw rectangles of size 7×4 cm and 8×3 cm
Calculate the area of both rectangles
Compare which rectangle has greater area

activities_projects.title: Triangle Area Investigation
steps: Draw a triangle on graph paper
Draw a rectangle around it
Compare the area of triangle and rectangle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that triangle area is half of rectangle

activities_projects.title: Parallelogram Transformation
steps: Draw a parallelogram on chart paper
Cut a triangular portion from one side
Attach it to the other side to form a rectangle

activities_projects.title: Rhombus Area Calculation
steps: Draw a rhombus with diagonals 10 cm and 8 cm
Use the formula 1/2 × d1 × d2
Calculate the area

activities_projects.title: Trapezium Area Activity
steps: Draw a trapezium with parallel sides 8 cm and 12 cm
Take height as 6 cm
Calculate the area

activities_projects.title: Polygon Area by Triangles
steps: Draw a pentagon
Divide it into triangles
Calculate total area by adding triangle areas

activities_projects.title: Perimeter vs Area
activities_projects.objective: Understand difference between perimeter and area
steps: Draw two shapes with same perimeter
Calculate their areas
Compare results

activities_projects.title: Square Area Expansion
steps: Draw a square of side 4 cm
Draw another square with side 8 cm
Calculate areas and compare

activities_projects.title: Area Estimation using A4 Sheets
steps: Measure a table or desk
Calculate its area
Estimate how many A4 sheets cover it

activities_projects.title: Diagonal Triangle Activity
steps: Draw a rectangle
Draw both diagonals
Compare areas of the four triangles formed
